title: CNN This Morning · 2026-07-09 · 美伊冲突与停火协议
summary: 美国与伊朗在停火协议后再次互相打击，美国称停火'暂时停止'，美军打击伊朗90个目标，伊朗报复袭击美军在科威特和巴林的基地。伊朗前最高领袖霍梅内伊葬礼接近尾声，其子穆杰塔巴未公开露面。德国外长表示愿在霍尔木兹海峡部署扫雷艇，但需各方同意。局势升级引发国际担忧。

paragraphs.speaker: Announcer
paragraphs.en: This is CNN Breaking News.
paragraphs.cn: 这里是CNN突发新闻。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: We're following this breaking news in the war with Iran after another night of strikes between the U.S. and Iran. Is the U.S. getting ready to return to full-scale war?
paragraphs.cn: 在美伊之间又一晚的打击之后，我们正在关注这场与伊朗的战争中的突发新闻。美国是否准备重返全面战争？

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.en: We just hit it very hard. And I'd say we hit it 20 to 1. Every time they hit us, we're going to hit them 20. And when they hit, we hit back much harder.
paragraphs.cn: 我们刚刚狠狠地打击了他们。我要说我们以20比1的比例还击。每次他们打击我们，我们都会以20倍的力量回击。当他们出手时，我们会以更猛烈的力度反击。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Good morning, everyone. I'm Audie Cornish. I want to start here. The uncertainty in the region this morning. A U.S. official telling CNN the ceasefire with Iran has, quote, temporarily ceased, but the situation remains dynamic. So, overnight, the U.S. military hit 90 targets in Iran. This is video obtained by CNN. You can see here showing some damage to what is a maritime control tower in a port city in the southeastern coast of Iran. Now, Iran claims the U.S. also hit a railway bridge northeast of Tehran.
paragraphs.cn: 大家早上好。我是奥黛·科尼什。我想从这里开始。今天上午该地区局势不确定。一位美国官员告诉CNN，与伊朗的停火协议"暂时停止"，但局势仍然动态变化。所以，一夜之间，美军打击了伊朗境内的90个目标。这是CNN获得的视频。你可以在这里看到，伊朗东南海岸一个港口城市的海上控制塔遭到了一些破坏。现在，伊朗声称美国还袭击了德黑兰东北部的一座铁路桥。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And if confirmed, this would be a rare case where civilian infrastructure was targeted. Iran's military says it targeted U.S. military bases in Kuwait and Bahrain. Their top negotiator posting this warning on X, quote, "America still hasn't learned that bullying and breaking promises are no longer cost- free. If you strike, you'll get hit." So we're bringing in CNN's Paula Hancocks joins us from Abu Dhabi. I want to start with the targets. We know that Iran has threatened to hit back.
paragraphs.cn: 如果得到证实，这将是罕见的针对民用基础设施的案例。伊朗军方表示，他们袭击了美国在科威特和巴林的军事基地。他们的首席谈判代表在X上发布了这一警告，引用如下："美国仍然没有学到，欺凌和背信弃义不再是没有代价的。如果你打击，你就会遭到打击。"所以我们请来了CNN的保拉·汉考克斯，她从阿布扎比加入我们。我想从目标开始。我们知道伊朗威胁要报复。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Do we have any sense of whether they were able to cause damage to U.S. military bases or civilian places in the region?
paragraphs.cn: 我们是否知道他们是否能够对该地区的美国军事基地或民用场所造成破坏？

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: So, Audie, we haven't had confirmation from those Gulf nations at this point as to exactly what damage there was, if there was any damage. But we do know it was a second night of sleeplessness for residents in both Bahrain and Kuwait. Bahrain saying that they had intercepted several attacks, calling it a flagrant violation of international humanitarian law. Iran also claiming that it targeted Qatar. in the retaliation against these U.S. military assets.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，奥黛，我们目前还没有从这些海湾国家得到确认，到底造成了什么破坏，如果有的话。但我们知道，对于巴林和科威特的居民来说，这是又一个不眠之夜。巴林表示，他们拦截了几次袭击，称这是公然违反国际人道主义法的行为。伊朗还声称，他们袭击了卡塔尔，作为对这些美国军事资产的报复。

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: Now, this is really the most serious escalation in violence that we've seen since this memorandum of understanding was signed. We are hearing from many around the world concerns that this will continue to escalate. I have a statement here from the United Nations spokesperson I want to read to you saying, quote, The Secretary General reiterates that a return to full-scale hostilities would have catastrophic consequences for the peoples of the region, for international peace and security, and for the global economy.
paragraphs.cn: 现在，这确实是自谅解备忘录签署以来我们所看到的最严重的暴力升级。我们听到世界各地许多人担心这种情况会继续升级。我这里有联合国发言人的一份声明，我想读给你们听，引用如下："秘书长重申，恢复全面敌对行动将对该地区人民、国际和平与安全以及全球经济造成灾难性后果。"

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: Now, it really is focusing around the Strait of Hormuz, starting when U.S. officials say that Iran targeted three vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, which sparked this 48 hours of U.S. strikes followed by Iranian retaliation. We are seeing the number of vessels managing to get through that critical waterway lesson because of the increase in hostilities. On Wednesday, we had some facts and figures from marine traffic saying that, in the previous 24 hours, about 20 ships had managed to get through.
paragraphs.cn: 现在，焦点确实集中在霍尔木兹海峡，始于美国官员称伊朗袭击了霍尔木兹海峡的三艘船只，这引发了美国48小时的打击，随后伊朗进行了报复。我们看到，由于敌对行动的加剧，能够通过这条关键水道的船只数量减少了。周三，我们从海上交通数据中了解到，在过去24小时内，大约有20艘船只成功通过。

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: Now, bear in mind, it's usually 110 per day at the pre-war level. So, certainly, we are seeing a significant impact on the region. We're also hearing from the U.N. Maritime Agency that they still have some 6,000 seafarers that is still stranded in the Persian Gulf since this war began, so many of them since February 28th, that they are unable to get out of this waterway at this point because of these hostilities that are ongoing -- Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 现在，请记住，战前水平通常是每天110艘。所以，我们确实看到对该地区产生了重大影响。我们还从联合国海事机构获悉，自这场战争开始以来，仍有大约6000名海员被困在波斯湾，其中许多人从2月28日起就无法离开这条水道，因为敌对行动仍在继续 -- -- 奥黛。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Paula, I do want to follow up on that last part because we're coming off a NATO summit. Al Jazeera just spoke to Germany's foreign minister, and he said that they were ready to at least take some supportive action in the Strait of Hormuz. I want you to take a listen.
paragraphs.cn: 宝拉，我想跟进最后那部分，因为我们刚开完北约峰会。半岛电视台刚刚采访了德国外长，他说他们准备至少在霍尔木兹海峡采取一些支持性行动。请你听一下。

paragraphs.speaker: Johanna Wadephul
paragraphs.en: And we have deployed a minesweeper in the region. We are ready to begin our work. But, of course, a permissive environment is needed. [05:05:2] So we need an agreement with Oman and with Iran that they agree that European units are doing this work. We are urging all parties to come back to the negotiation table.
paragraphs.cn: 我们已经在当地部署了一艘扫雷艇。我们准备开始工作。但当然，需要一个允许的环境。所以我们需要与阿曼和伊朗达成协议，让他们同意欧洲部队执行这项任务。我们敦促各方回到谈判桌上来。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So does that mean that these minesweepers can't get to work unless there is another ceasefire?
paragraphs.cn: 那么这是否意味着，除非再次停火，否则这些扫雷艇无法开始工作？

paragraphs.speaker: Hancocks
paragraphs.en: Well, certainly, there are very few countries who are going to want to put their carriers, their personnel, in the middle of what is happening at the moment. These mines are believed to be planted by Iran in the central route through the Strait of Hormuz. There is still a northern route, which hugs the Iranian coast, but that is the route where you have to have Iranian permission and coordination. Now, many vessels don't want to do that.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，当然，很少有国家愿意把他们的航母和人员置于当前局势之中。这些水雷据信是伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡的中央航道上布设的。还有一条北部航道，紧贴伊朗海岸，但那条航道需要获得伊朗的许可和协调。现在，许多船只不愿意那样做。

paragraphs.speaker: Hancocks
paragraphs.en: So they are using the southern route of this Strait of Hormuz, so hugging the Omani coast. And that is where this problem lies as far as Iran is concerned. They believe that they are in control of the Strait of Hormuz. They believe that this memorandum gives them control over the logistics, the coordination, and all ships transiting through have to coordinate with them and any that are trying to avoid that and go via the Omani route, some of them are being targeted.
paragraphs.cn: 所以他们使用霍尔木兹海峡的南部航道，紧贴阿曼海岸。而就伊朗而言，问题就在这里。他们认为自己控制着霍尔木兹海峡。他们认为这份备忘录赋予他们控制物流和协调的权力，所有过往船只都必须与他们协调，任何试图避开这一点而走阿曼航道的船只，其中一些会成为目标。

paragraphs.speaker: Hancocks
paragraphs.en: We know a Saudi tanker, a Qatari tanker earlier this week were both targeted. So when it comes to this central route, it's simply not being used by many vessels at this point, because that is where the mines are believed to be. But as I say, it's hard to believe that any country, any of these European nations offering the mine-sweeping capabilities are going to want to put their vessels and their personnel in the middle of this tit-for-tat that we are seeing at this exact moment -- Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 我们知道一艘沙特油轮和一辆卡塔尔油轮本周早些时候都成为了目标。所以说到这条中央航道，目前很多船只根本不使用它，因为那里据信有水雷。但正如我所说，很难相信任何国家，任何提供扫雷能力的欧洲国家，会愿意把他们的船只和人员置于我们此刻看到的这种针锋相对之中 -- -- 奥迪。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, that's Paula Hancock speaking to us from Abu Dhabi. We want to talk about what's been happening in Iran domestically. This new round of strikes is coming as the week-long funeral procession for Iran's former supreme leader nears an end. Earlier today, we were talking about this marathon funeral continued in Iraq, where mourners carried Ali Khamenei's coffin in a crowded mosque. His coffin is now being taken to its final resting place, the town where he was born in Iran.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，这是宝拉·汉考克从阿布扎比发回的报道。我们来谈谈伊朗国内的情况。新一轮罢工发生之际，伊朗前最高领袖为期一周的葬礼即将结束。今天早些时候，我们谈到这场马拉松式的葬礼在伊拉克继续进行，哀悼者在一个拥挤的清真寺里抬着阿里·哈梅内伊的棺材。他的棺材现在正被运往最后的安息地，他在伊朗出生的城镇。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The question looming over today's events, will the new Supreme Leader, Khamenei's son, make an appearance? So, CNN's Fred Pleitgen is in Tehran. CNN is operating inside the country with permission of the government, and CNN retains full editorial control of our reporting. Fred, I want to start with the former leader being finally laid to rest. The question has been, given the threats to Iran's leadership, whether his son Mojtaba would be there today, and what's the sense of whether that could happen?
paragraphs.cn: 今天活动的一个悬而未决的问题是，新最高领袖哈梅内伊的儿子会露面吗？CNN的弗雷德·普莱特根在德黑兰。CNN在政府许可下在伊朗境内进行报道，CNN保留对我们报道的完全编辑控制权。弗雷德，我想从这位前领袖最终安葬开始。问题一直是，鉴于伊朗领导层受到的威胁，他的儿子穆杰塔巴今天是否会到场，以及这种可能性有多大？

paragraphs.speaker: Frederik Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: Hi there, Audie. Well, I think it's highly unlikely that Mojtaba Khamenei is going to make an appearance in Mashhad. You're absolutely right. He hasn't been seen since taking the reins of the Islamic Republic of Iran. He's really only communicated by our written statement, so we don't even have any audio messages from him. One of the things, though, that the leadership here says is they say that he's fully in charge, he's fully in control.
paragraphs.cn: 你好，奥迪。嗯，我认为穆杰塔巴·哈梅内伊在马什哈德露面的可能性非常小。你说得完全正确。自从接管伊朗伊斯兰共和国以来，他就没有出现过。他实际上只通过书面声明进行沟通，所以我们甚至没有他的任何音频信息。不过，这里的领导层说的一件事是，他们说他完全掌控局面，完全在控制之中。

paragraphs.speaker: Frederik Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: Certainly, if you ask the supporters of the Islamic Republic of Iran, and many of them, of course, were out here on the streets over the past couple of days as this marathon funeral ceremonies were going on here in Tehran and other cities in Iran, as you mentioned, also all the way to Iraq and the holiest Shiite cities there, finally ending in Mashhad in the east of Iran today, that many people came out. They were out in force. They were very angry at the United States.
paragraphs.cn: 当然，如果你问伊朗伊斯兰共和国的支持者，他们中的许多人，当然，在过去几天里，当这场马拉松式的葬礼在德黑兰和伊朗其他城市举行时，正如你提到的，一直延伸到伊拉克和最神圣的什叶派城市，最后今天在伊朗东部的马什哈德结束，很多人都出来了。他们大量出现，对美国非常愤怒。

paragraphs.speaker: Frederik Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: They called for revenge for the killing of Ali Khamenei. Essentially, what the people here are saying is whether or not Mojtaba Khamenei makes an appearance, which seems pretty unlikely at this point in time, that he is the one calling the shots, that he is also the one directing Iran's reaction, for instance, to those U.S. strikes and also to all the negotiations that are going on with the U.S. as well.
paragraphs.cn: 他们呼吁为阿里·哈梅内伊之死复仇。本质上，这里的人们在说的是，无论穆杰塔巴·哈梅内伊是否露面 -- -- 目前看来这似乎不太可能 -- -- 他才是发号施令的人，也是指挥伊朗反应的人，例如对美国空袭以及与美国进行的所有谈判的反应。

paragraphs.speaker: Frederik Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: So, they say, whether or not he's seen in public, he certainly is a big presence as far as running the Islamic Republic is concerned. And one of the things, Audie, by the way, that we also have to keep in mind is that the office of Supreme Leader is actually a pretty large body. It's also a big administration as well.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，他们说，无论他是否在公开场合露面，就管理伊斯兰共和国而言，他无疑是一个重要人物。顺便提一下，奥迪，我们还需要记住的一点是，最高领袖办公室实际上是一个相当庞大的机构，也是一个庞大的行政体系。

paragraphs.speaker: Frederik Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: So in any case, that seems to be functioning in a way that at least is effective enough to control those negotiations, but then also, of course, to oversee the ceremonies that we're seeing there happening in Mashhad, which is, of course, the holiest place in Shia Islam on Iranian soil -- Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 所以无论如何，这个体系似乎至少在有效运作，足以控制那些谈判，当然也监督我们在马什哈德看到的那些仪式 -- -- 马什哈德是伊朗境内什叶派伊斯兰教最神圣的地方 -- -- 奥迪。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: For people who aren't familiar with how this is supposed to work, why the length of time between the Supreme Leader's death and these funeral proceedings?
paragraphs.cn: 对于那些不熟悉这一流程的人来说，为什么最高领袖去世到葬礼之间会有这么长的时间间隔？

paragraphs.speaker: Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: Well, that's -- yeah, that's basically due to the war that was going on between the United States, Israel, and then Iran on the other side. You'll recall that Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in the early hours of the U.S. and Israel's attacks on Iran in a massive airstrike here in Tehran on his headquarters in the center of the city on February 28. Of course, the Iranians have been vowing revenge, but then the actual kinetic operations between the U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，这 -- -- 是的，这主要是因为美国、以色列和伊朗之间正在进行的战争。你可能还记得，阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊是在美国和以色列对伊朗发动袭击的凌晨被杀的，当时他们在德黑兰市中心对他的总部进行了大规模空袭，时间是2月28日。当然，伊朗人一直誓言复仇，但随后美国

paragraphs.speaker: Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: and Iran, and of course, Israel on the U.S.'s side went on for an extended period of time, and the Iranians simply felt, that it was too dangerous and logistically difficult to conduct the large funeral ceremonies that they wanted to conduct. And if you look at what's been going on here over the past couple of days, it's been one event after another. I think you called it a marathon funeral ceremony, and that certainly is what it was.
paragraphs.cn: 和伊朗之间，当然还有美国一方的以色列，实际的军事行动持续了相当长一段时间，伊朗人只是觉得，举行他们想要的那种大型葬礼仪式太危险，后勤也太困难。如果你看看过去几天这里发生的事情，那是一个接一个的活动。我想你称之为马拉松式的葬礼，确实如此。

paragraphs.speaker: Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: The body, not just of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, because some members of his family were also killed in that stripe, including a 14-month-old granddaughter. They lie in state for two days. Then there was a very big procession here in Tehran. Then the body was brought to a very important holy site in Iran, then to the two main Shia holy sites, Najaf and Karbala, on Iraqi soil, and then finally today to Mashhad in the east of Iran.
paragraphs.cn: 遗体不仅包括阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊，因为他的部分家人也在那次袭击中丧生，包括一个14个月大的孙女。他们停灵两天。然后在德黑兰举行了一次非常盛大的游行。随后遗体被运往伊朗一个非常重要的圣地，然后到伊拉克境内的两个主要什叶派圣地纳杰夫和卡尔巴拉，最后今天到达伊朗东部的马什哈德。

paragraphs.speaker: Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: So, as you can imagine, this is a huge logistical operation on the part of the authorities, on the part of organizations that are pitching in as well. And then, of course, also big logistic operation as far as people traveling to those places on planes, trains, and automobiles, that that is something that required a lot of operation by the authorities. And they simply said they didn't have the capacity to do that, as that ceasefire between the U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，可以想象，这对当局和参与其中的组织来说是一项巨大的后勤行动。当然，对于人们乘坐飞机、火车和汽车前往这些地方来说，也是一项巨大的后勤行动，这需要当局进行大量运作。他们只是说，他们没有能力做到这一点，因为美国

paragraphs.speaker: Pleitgen
paragraphs.en: and Iran, of course, was extremely shaky before the signing of that memorandum of understanding. And now we're seeing it seems shaky once again -- Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 和伊朗之间的停火在签署谅解备忘录之前非常不稳定。现在我们看到它似乎再次变得不稳定 -- -- 奥迪。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: One other thing, I've been reading that there are people with signs in English there, and that there have been calls for revenge within the crowd. Can you talk about the mood in and around the cities as they are going through this process? Sure, absolutely. And you're absolutely right. There are a lot of people with signs in English calling for revenge against the United States, revenge against Israel, revenge specifically against U.S. President Donald Trump.
paragraphs.cn: 还有一件事，我读到那里有人举着英文标语，人群中有人呼吁复仇。你能谈谈城市内外的情绪吗？当然可以。你说得完全正确。有很多人举着英文标语，呼吁对美国复仇，对以色列复仇，特别是对美国唐纳德·特朗普总统复仇。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And a lot of those signs are obviously meant to show that anger also to Western audiences and to U.S. audiences as well. I can tell you from anywhere that we've turned up at these funeral processions, people were very keen to show us those signs. People in, you know, even if they didn't speak very good English, in English, told us that they would have revenge on the United States and on U.S. President Donald Trump.
paragraphs.cn: 而且很多标语显然也是为了向西方观众和美国观众表达愤怒。我可以告诉你，无论我们出现在这些葬礼游行队伍的哪个地方，人们都非常热衷于向我们展示这些标语。即使有些人英语说得不好，他们也会用英语告诉我们，他们将对美国和美国总统唐纳德·特朗普进行报复。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So that whole notion of revenge is one that is extremely important to the supporters of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and they certainly did come out in force here on the ground over the past couple of days. We have to caveat that, of course, there are people here in this country who opposed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's role and his rule.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，复仇这个概念对阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊的支持者来说极其重要，而且过去几天他们确实大规模地出现在这里。当然，我们必须说明，这个国家也有人反对阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊的角色和他的统治。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But we did see over the past few days, as these processions were going on, that the government here was able to bring out people in force with many hundreds of thousands of people coming on the streets, specifically for that procession that happened in Tehran and really voiced their support.
paragraphs.cn: 但在过去几天，随着这些游行队伍的前进，我们看到政府能够动员大量人群，数十万人走上街头，特别是在德黑兰举行的游行中，他们真正表达了支持。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And I think that it -- the power, the people who are in power here in this country certainly see that as also shoring up the support that they have as they are in these negotiations with the United States and now, again, in that standoff with the United States as well -- Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 而且我认为，这个国家的掌权者当然也认为这巩固了他们的支持，因为他们正在与美国进行谈判，现在又再次与美国陷入对峙 -- -- 奥黛。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, that's our Fred Pleitgen, joining us. He's live in Tehran. Thank you so much. And coming up on CNN THIS MORNING, we're going to talk about the strategy out of Tehran, whether they are trying to box President Trump in, keep the clock ticking until the U.S. midterm election day. Plus, President Trump ditches the new Air Force One for at least one leg of his trip home. We're going to find out what's behind that switch up. And in the state of Maine, Graham Platner is out of the Senate race.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，那是我们的弗雷德·普莱特根，他正在德黑兰进行直播。非常感谢。接下来在《CNN今晨》节目中，我们将讨论德黑兰的策略，他们是否试图将特朗普总统逼入困境，拖延时间直到美国中期选举日。此外，特朗普总统在回国途中至少有一段行程放弃了新的空军一号。我们将了解这一变化背后的原因。在缅因州，格雷厄姆·普拉特纳退出了参议员竞选。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Democrats have to find a new candidate in one of their most important races of the midterms. GRAHAM PLATNER (D),
paragraphs.cn: 民主党人必须在其中期选举最重要的竞选之一中找到新的候选人。格雷厄姆·普拉特纳（民主党），

paragraphs.speaker: Former Maine Senate Candidate
paragraphs.en: Accusations are supposed to be the beginning of things, not the end.
paragraphs.cn: 指控应该是事情的开始，而不是结束。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, when President Trump left Turkey for England on the first leg of his trip home, he was on the old Air Force One instead of his new Qatari-donated plane. And the switch came at the urging of the Secret Service. A source tells CNN they were concerned about the security capabilities of the new aircraft in international settings. The president claims that he sent the Qatari-gifted jet to Milton Hall, Air Force Base in the U.K. to give our soldiers, quote, "a chance to see it".
paragraphs.cn: 因此，当特朗普总统离开土耳其前往英国，作为他回国行程的第一站时，他乘坐的是旧的空军一号，而不是卡塔尔捐赠的新飞机。这一改变是在特勤局的敦促下进行的。一位消息人士告诉CNN，他们担心新飞机在国际环境中的安全能力。总统声称，他将卡塔尔赠送的飞机送到了英国的米尔顿霍尔空军基地，以便让我们的士兵"有机会看看它"。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But that's left reporters with a lot of questions.
paragraphs.cn: 但这给记者留下了许多疑问。

paragraphs.en: Do you think there wasn't a security concern?
paragraphs.cn: 你认为没有安全问题吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.en: No, no. Why would --
paragraphs.cn: 不，不。为什么要 -- --

paragraphs.en: Do you know why they had us close our window blinds? That was not usual.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道他们为什么让我们关上窗帘吗？这可不寻常。

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.en: Well, yeah, because you're probably on a dangerous flight because of the sleazebags that we have to deal with.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，是啊，因为你可能正乘坐一趟危险的航班，因为我们得对付那些卑鄙小人。

paragraphs.en: Iran was possibly thinking --
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗可能在想 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.en: Yeah. I mean, if they ask you to close your windows, probably they feel that way. They didn't ask me to close mine, but if they did, I would have done it.
paragraphs.cn: 是啊。我是说，如果他们让你关上窗户，很可能他们就是这么想的。他们没让我关，但如果他们让我关，我会照做的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So we're bringing in Becca Wasser, defense lead at "Bloomberg Economics". Becca, we've talked about this plane in the past, what it would cost to retrofit it. And now we are here, right, in the moment when it has to travel internationally. And I want you to help clarify a thing. I mean, sometimes the flight attendant tells you to put the shades down because of the sun. So what do you hear between this conversation of? Is it a security concern or something else?
paragraphs.cn: 现在我们请来贝卡·瓦瑟，彭博经济防务板块负责人。贝卡，我们过去讨论过这架飞机，以及改装它的成本。而现在，它真的需要执行国际飞行任务了。我想请你帮忙澄清一件事。我的意思是，有时空乘人员会因为阳光让你放下遮光板。那么你从这段对话中听出了什么？是安全问题还是别的什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Becca Wasser
paragraphs.en: Listen, I think what's happening here is the efforts to try and retrofit this aircraft so quickly means that there are some gaps in what it needs to be a secure Air Force One for an international visit. And what I'm hearing between all of this is really the lack of three things that caused him to fly home on the older Air Force One. And it's about security. It's about communications and it's about survivability.
paragraphs.cn: 听着，我认为现在的情况是，试图如此迅速地改装这架飞机意味着，它要成为一架用于国际访问的安全的空军一号，还存在一些不足。我从这一切中听到的，实际上是导致他乘坐旧空军一号回国的三个缺失要素。那就是安全性、通信能力和生存能力。

paragraphs.speaker: Becca Wasser
paragraphs.en: So if, you know, there was this rush to trying get the Qatari aircraft to be his Air Force One, that means that it's probably lacking some of its missile detection and countermeasures that it likely needs. Additionally, it probably doesn't have the full secure communications network that President Trump would also need to essentially prosecute a war from afar with some of the strikes on Iran that we've seen in the past two days. And then lastly, it probably lacks some of the basic survivability that it would need.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，如果急于让这架卡塔尔飞机成为他的空军一号，那它可能缺少所需的导弹探测和对抗措施。此外，它可能没有完整的保密通信网络，而特朗普总统需要这些网络来从远方指挥战争，就像过去两天我们看到对伊朗的打击那样。最后，它可能缺乏一些基本的生存能力。

paragraphs.speaker: Becca Wasser
paragraphs.en: Some of those Air Force One airplanes really were built to be survivable under even the worst things like being able to survive a potential nuclear blast. And it is very clear that the Qatari jet definitely lacks that capability.
paragraphs.cn: 有些空军一号飞机确实被设计成在最坏情况下也能生存，比如能够承受潜在的核爆炸。很明显，这架卡塔尔飞机绝对缺乏这种能力。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Becca, I'm sorry, this is crazy. These are the three things that are the point of an Air Force One. I mean, security, communications, and survivability. I mean, was this kind of inevitable? I had always wondered, would it have to be gutted to the studs to be what the U.S. needs it to be.
paragraphs.cn: 贝卡，抱歉，这太疯狂了。这三样东西正是空军一号的核心。我是说，安全性、通信能力和生存能力。这是否不可避免？我一直想知道，它是不是得被彻底拆空才能达到美国的要求。

paragraphs.speaker: Wasser
paragraphs.en: I think it probably had to, but that wasn't the timeline that, frankly, the Air Force was offered. So they managed to do this under a year, and that could possibly work for domestic use of Air Force One. But when the president is flying overseas in international airspace under different conditions, you're going to want something that is a little bit more secure than what that could offer. And I think that's exactly why we're seeing him fly home on some of the older jets.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为可能不得不这样做，但坦率地说，空军当时并没有得到这样的时间表。所以他们在一年内完成了这项工作，这可能适用于空军一号的国内使用。但当总统在海外国际空域飞行时，条件不同，你需要比这更安全的东西。我认为这正是我们看到他用一些较旧的飞机飞回家的原因。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: In the end, is that kind of what this is for, though, his domestic comfort?
paragraphs.cn: 说到底，这难道只是为了他在国内的舒适吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Wasser
paragraphs.en: It could be, but I think it was very clear that Qatar offered a gift to the president, and it was one that he wanted to take. And so, you know, the U.S. military was essentially given a job to do, which is make this work. And what they've done is tried to make it work. But that doesn't necessarily mean that it meets the standard that is required for the president's overseas travel and to be able to do some of the secure -- you know, messages that he would probably need to do.
paragraphs.cn: 可能是，但我认为很明显卡塔尔向总统提供了一份礼物，而他也想接受。所以，你知道，美国军方基本上接到了一个任务，就是让这件事成功。他们所做的就是试图让它成功。但这并不一定意味着它符合总统海外旅行所需的标准，以及能够进行一些他可能需要做的安全通信。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So what's at stake here? We are in the middle of renewed hostilities with Iran. Trump flew on this Air Force One as this was escalating. After that, they switched planes. Is there a concern, you think, about Iran specifically, or, as we said, this kind of general concern about international, you know, support for the leader of the free world?
paragraphs.cn: 那么这里的关键是什么？我们正处于与伊朗重新敌对的状态中。特朗普在局势升级时乘坐了这架空军一号。之后，他们换了飞机。你认为是否存在对伊朗的特别担忧，或者像我们说的，对自由世界领导人的国际支持这种普遍担忧？

paragraphs.speaker: Wasser
paragraphs.en: I think it's more about general concerns and, again, having that secure communications network that you would need to be able to, you know, help, you know, develop military operations and make those calls. Iran lacks capabilities with the correct ranges to be able to come after some of, you know, some of where the president would be. You know, it's -- Iranian drones are short-range. They aren't able to get to Turkey.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为更多的是普遍担忧，以及再次需要那种安全的通信网络，以便能够帮助制定军事行动并进行那些通话。伊朗缺乏适当射程的能力来攻击总统可能所在的一些地方。你知道，伊朗无人机是短程的，它们无法到达土耳其。

paragraphs.speaker: Wasser
paragraphs.en: Additionally, while it does have missiles that have greater range, that's probably not, you know, what the biggest concern was here, even though it is definitely something that, you know, I'm sure the Secret Service has been very concerned about.
paragraphs.cn: 此外，虽然它确实有射程更远的导弹，但这可能不是这里最大的担忧，尽管我确信特勤局对此非常担心。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, Becca Wasser, thank you for talking with us about this. I know you will be back as we talk about this fresh round of strikes on Iran. And after the break on CNN THIS MORNING, President Trump has lost again in court. There's a judge here who says that E. Jean Carroll is owed millions of dollars. But will she ever collect? Plus, who has the leverage in this Mideast standoff, the balance of power in the U.S. war with Iran?
paragraphs.cn: 好的，贝卡·瓦瑟，感谢你与我们讨论这个问题。我知道当我们讨论对伊朗的新一轮打击时，你还会回来。在CNN今晨节目休息后，特朗普总统在法庭上再次败诉。一位法官表示E·简·卡罗尔应获得数百万美元。但她能拿到吗？另外，在这场中东对峙中，谁拥有筹码，美国与伊朗战争中的力量平衡如何？

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.en: They have very little left, and they want to make a deal so badly. They called a little while ago. They want to make a deal so badly. I just don't know if they're worthy of making a deal. I don't know that they're going to honor the deal.
paragraphs.cn: 他们几乎没有什么了，他们非常想达成协议。他们刚才打电话来了。他们非常想达成协议。我只是不知道他们是否值得达成协议。我不知道他们是否会遵守协议。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So the president claims Iran now wants to make a deal as the two countries exchanged a new round of strikes. U.S. Central Command saying they carried out attacks on 90 military targets across Iran. Iran's Revolutionary Guard saying it responded with drones and missiles aimed at U.S. military bases in Kuwait and Bahrain. So, at this point, who has the upper hand in making a deal?
paragraphs.cn: 所以总统声称伊朗现在想达成协议，而两国刚刚交换了一轮新的打击。美国中央司令部表示他们对伊朗境内的90个军事目标进行了攻击。伊朗革命卫队表示他们用无人机和导弹回应，目标是美国在科威特和巴林军事基地。那么，在这一点上，谁在达成协议方面占上风？

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Vance
paragraphs.en: We came into the situation with maximum leverage and maximum strength. Their nuclear program was destroyed. Their conventional military was destroyed. And you've got some people within the Iranian system who are saying, you know what? We want to change our relationship with the United States. We want to turn over a new leaf. And the president said, OK, we will sit down at the table and negotiate and see if the smart people in that system can take over from the crazy people.
paragraphs.cn: 我们进入局势时拥有最大的筹码和最大的力量。他们的核计划被摧毁了。他们的常规军事力量被摧毁了。伊朗系统内有一些人在说，你知道吗？我们想改变与美国的关系。我们想翻开新的一页。总统说，好吧，我们将坐下来谈判，看看那个系统中的聪明人能否从疯子手中接管。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Joining me now, Sabrina Singh, CNN global affairs commentator, also former deputy Pentagon press secretary. Sabrina, good morning. I want to start with what the President was saying there, talking about maximum leverage, because we heard him describe it as leverage militarily. Can you help me parse that with where we are now, the U.S. having to do strikes again? var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 现在加入我们的是萨布丽娜·辛格，CNN全球事务评论员，前五角大楼副新闻秘书。萨布丽娜，早上好。我想从总统刚才说的开始，他提到了最大杠杆，因为我们听到他将其描述为军事杠杆。你能帮我解析一下这与我们现在的处境 -- -- 美国不得不再次发动打击 -- -- 之间的关系吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Joining me now is Sabrina Singh, CNN global affairs commentator and also former deputy Pentagon press secretary. Sabrina, good morning. I want to start with what the president was saying there, talking about maximum leverage because we heard him describe it as leverage militarily. Can you help me parse that with where we are now -- the U.S. having to do strikes again? SABRINA SINGH, CNN GLOBAL AFFAIRS COMMENTATOR, FORMER DEPUTY PENTAGON PRESS SECRETARY (via Webex by Cisco): Yeah. Good morning, Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 现在加入我们的是萨布丽娜·辛格，CNN全球事务评论员，前五角大楼副新闻秘书。萨布丽娜，早上好。我想从总统刚才说的开始，他提到了最大杠杆，因为我们听到他将其描述为军事杠杆。你能帮我解析一下这与我们现在的处境 -- -- 美国不得不再次发动打击 -- -- 之间的关系吗？萨布丽娜·辛格，CNN全球事务评论员，前五角大楼副新闻秘书（通过Webex by Cisco）：是的。早上好，奥迪。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: The United States military does retain significant capabilities within the Middle East. I mean, we have an aircraft carrier there with multiple destroyers that can continue to launch waves of strikes that you saw last night and the night before on Iran. So there is military leverage, but can Iran be bombed into submission over time? And I think the answer that we know to that is no. Just look at the course of the war and how it has played out. This war started February 28.
paragraphs.cn: 美国军方在中东确实保留了强大的能力。我的意思是，我们在那里有一艘航空母舰和多艘驱逐舰，可以继续发动像昨晚和前晚对伊朗那样的打击波次。所以存在军事杠杆，但伊朗能被炸到屈服吗？我认为我们知道答案是否定的。看看战争的过程以及它是如何发展的就知道了。这场战争始于2月28日。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: President Trump said then that this was about to be, you know, around four to six weeks. Obviously that timeline has just blown by. We're almost at mid-July at this point and the war is continuing. So the United States retains some military capabilities which allows it some leverage, but it does have economic leverage and that really comes in the form of sanctions. And before the sanctions were lifted on Iran and Iran was able to sell its oil on the global market its economy was cratering. So the U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普总统当时说这大约需要四到六周。显然，这个时间表已经被打破了。现在快到七月中旬了，战争仍在继续。所以美国保留了一些军事能力，这给了它一些杠杆，但它也有经济杠杆，这实际上是以制裁的形式出现的。在制裁解除之前，伊朗无法在全球市场上出售石油，其经济正在崩溃。所以美国重新实施了一些制裁。我们仍然掌握着一些牌，就像总统喜欢用的那个词。但总体而言，伊朗确实有时间优势，他们可以继续拖延时间。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: has put some of those sanctions back in place. We still hold some of those cards, like the president likes to use that term. But overall Iran really has time on its side, and they can continue to run down the clock.
paragraphs.cn: 现在，在众议院，民主党众议员亚当·史密斯，他是军事委员会成员 -- -- 当然，他一直批评总统最初发动战争的方式和原因。但这是他对于伊朗最新举动的看法。众议员亚当·史密斯（华盛顿州民主党）：但我要说，我认为伊朗有点在挑战运气。我的意思是，他们本来有一个相当不错的协议，可以收取费用来开放海峡。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, in the House, Democratic Congressman Adam Smith, who is on the Armed Services committee -- he, of course, has been critical of how and why the president started the war in the first place. But this is what he had to say about the latest move from Iran. REP. ADAM SMITH (D-WASHINGTON): But I will say I think Iran is kind of pushing their luck a little bit. I mean, they had a pretty good deal here where they could get paid to open the strait.
paragraphs.cn: 相反，他们想说，好吧，我们会开放海峡一点，但不是完全开放。这就是为什么他们向一些通过他们不希望船只通过的航道的船只开火。现在我认为我们正在面临一场旷日持久的冲突，谈判在一段时间内将陷入困境。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Instead they wanted to say well, we'll open the strait a little bit, not entirely. And that's why they're shooting at some ships going through the lanes they don't want them to go through. Now I think we're looking at a protracted conflict, and the negotiations are going to be in trouble for a while.
paragraphs.cn: 你能谈谈这个吗？我在其他地方也听说过，伊朗在过去几天对海峡的打击，本质上是在把船只推向他们希望船只通过的航道，因为他们希望有一天能收取通行费。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can you talk about this? I've heard this in other places that Iran, in its strikes in the strait in the last couple of days, was essentially nudging people into the lane that it wanted them to traverse because they hoped to one day have a toll.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。这就是美国和伊朗签署的谅解备忘录的问题所在。其细节非常模糊，在备忘录的其中一行中确实提到伊朗负责清除海峡中的水雷。现在，伊朗显然将其解释为它控制着霍尔木兹海峡。而且因为细节 -- -- 我的意思是，有14点，1页半。我认为我们讨论过比备忘录还长的购物清单。

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: Yeah. This is the problem with the MOU that was signed between the United States and Iran. It was very murky in its details and in one of the lines in the MOU it does say that Iran is responsible for clearing the strait of mines. Now, Iran is obviously interpreting that as it controls the Strait of Hormuz. And because the details -- I mean, there was 14 points, 1 1/2 pages. I mean, I think we've talked about having grocery lists that are longer than the MOU.
paragraphs.cn: 双方对备忘录以及如何执行都会存在误解。但史密斯众议员是对的。我的意思是，备忘录在很大程度上有利于伊朗。他们能够用美元在全球市场上出售石油，所以这对他们来说是一笔非常好的交易。现在他们正在展示一些力量。他们试图投射力量，因为他们袭击了一些过境的船只。他们希望船只向他们支付某种费用或通行费。

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: There's going to be misinterpretations on both sides of the MOU and how it should be executed. But Congressman Smith is right. I mean, the MOU hugely, largely favored Iran. They were able to sell their oil on the global market in American dollars and so this was a very good deal for them. Now they're exerting some power. They're trying to project power as they've attacked some of those ships transiting. They want ships to pay some type of fee or toll to them.

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: And the United States is basically saying that goes against freedom of navigation international law and the MOU that you signed. But this is really the problem. Both sides signed a very murky agreement and now to implement it and execute it it's proving to be very difficult as we're seeing kinetic action over the last few days.
paragraphs.cn: 美国基本上是在说，这违反了航行自由、国际法以及你们签署的谅解备忘录。但这才是真正的问题所在。双方签署了一份非常模糊的协议，而现在要执行和实施它，事实证明非常困难，正如我们过去几天看到的激烈行动。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: What can you tell us when you look at the targets, meaning the U.S. focusing on these coastal parts? You've got Iran at least aiming in the direction of U.S. military bases. When will we know that this is quite serious based on the target?
paragraphs.cn: 当你观察目标时，能告诉我们什么？我的意思是，美国专注于这些沿海地区。伊朗至少将目标指向了美国军事基地。基于目标，我们什么时候才能知道事态相当严重？

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: Well, what is interesting is also Iran launched strikes at Qatar, and we know Qatar has been a main interlocutor when it comes to securing this peace agreement. So it's a little confusing why they're also antagonizing a party that is trying to bring both sides to the table, but they are saying that they're targeting U.S. bases in those three countries. Now, the U.S. military is targeting military sites that they're saying are in the southeast of Iran.
paragraphs.cn: 有趣的是，伊朗还对卡塔尔发动了袭击，而我们知道卡塔尔在促成这项和平协议方面一直是主要调解方。所以有点令人困惑，为什么他们还要激怒一个试图让双方回到谈判桌的当事方，但他们说他们正在攻击这三个国家的美国基地。现在，美国军方正在打击他们所说的位于伊朗东南部的军事地点。

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: These are the sites that are really being used to control the Strait of Hormuz. I think when it gets beyond those sites along the strait and we see strikes within central Iran -- the western part of the country -- that's when we're going to see a larger expansion of this war. I really don't think either side wants to go back to a full-scale war. It is just depleting of not only just munitions but time and energy. I think both sides do want to see some type of agreement and ceasefire put back in place.
paragraphs.cn: 这些地点实际上是用来控制霍尔木兹海峡的。我认为，当打击范围超出海峡沿岸的这些地点，我们看到伊朗中部 -- -- 该国西部地区 -- -- 遭到袭击时，那将意味着这场战争进一步扩大。我真的认为双方都不想回到全面战争。这不仅消耗弹药，还消耗时间和精力。我认为双方都希望看到某种协议和停火得以恢复。

paragraphs.speaker: Singh
paragraphs.en: But it is very possible that this war expands and then you're going to have Gulf countries brought back into this. And they were really the ones that said to this administration we need to stop this war and put some type of ceasefire into place because we just can't handle the level of strikes that are coming towards our country right now.
paragraphs.cn: 但这场战争很有可能扩大，然后海湾国家将再次被卷入。而他们正是那些对现政府说我们需要停止这场战争并实施某种停火的人，因为他们现在无法承受针对自己国家的打击强度。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yeah. It'll be interesting to see what we hear from those countries out of the region today. Sabrina Singh, thank you for being here. Appreciate your time.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。今天听听这些地区国家的声音将会很有趣。萨布丽娜·辛格，感谢你的到来。谢谢你的时间。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING we're going to turn to that high-rise in Manhattan that had seemed on the very of collapse. Now investigators want to know how did things get to that point. And coming up in the next hour, has Israel become a dividing line for Democrats? We're going to talk about why Rahm Emanuel says the party needs a new strategy. (COMMERCIAL)
paragraphs.cn: 现在，在CNN《今晨》节目中，我们将关注曼哈顿那栋似乎濒临倒塌的高楼。现在调查人员想知道事情是如何发展到这一步的。在接下来的一个小时里，以色列是否已成为民主党的分界线？我们将讨论为什么拉姆·伊曼纽尔说该党需要新策略。（广告）

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's now almost 40 minutes past the hour. This is your Morning Roundup. Democrats in Maine are scrambling to find a new candidate for Senate as Graham Platner drops out of the race.
paragraphs.cn: 现在距离整点还有将近40分钟。这是你的晨间简报。缅因州的民主党人正在争相寻找新的参议院候选人，因为格雷厄姆·普拉特纳退出了竞选。

paragraphs.speaker: Graham Platner
paragraphs.en: I intend to file my paperwork to withdraw. What comes next needs to come from the people. It needs to come from the people of Maine.
paragraphs.cn: 我打算提交文件退出。接下来该怎么办需要由人民决定。需要由缅因州人民决定。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now this all comes after allegations of sexual misconduct from several women, which caused a mass exodus of endorsements and contributions to his campaign. Platner denies the accusations. The Maine Democratic Party will hold a nominating convention to select a new Senate nominee, and they have until July 27 to make that decision. And a federal judge orders the release of nearly $6 million to E. Jean Carroll from President Trump. His team immediately appealed the decision. They were denied.
paragraphs.cn: 这一切发生在几名女性提出性行为不端指控之后，这些指控导致大量背书和捐款流失。普拉特纳否认这些指控。缅因州民主党将举行提名大会，选出新的参议院候选人，他们必须在7月27日之前做出决定。一名联邦法官下令向E·简·卡罗尔支付近600万美元，来自特朗普总统。他的团队立即对该决定提出上诉，但被驳回。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: President Trump has been trying to delay paying Carroll after he was found liable for sexually abusing and defaming her. In New York, there is significant progress in the stabilization of a Manhattan high-rise that had been at risk of collapse. Crews added steel supports to each floor from the ninth to the roof. The New York Department of Buildings has inspectors and engineers on site monitoring and investigating what caused the structural failure earlier this week.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普总统在因性侵和诽谤卡罗尔被判有罪后，一直试图拖延支付赔偿金。在纽约，一座曾面临倒塌风险的曼哈顿高层建筑的稳定工作取得重大进展。工作人员从九层到屋顶的每一层都增加了钢支撑。纽约市建筑局已派检查员和工程师在现场监测并调查本周早些时候结构失效的原因。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And next on CNN THIS MORNING what's life like on the front lines of the war with Iran? We take you aboard a U.S. aircraft carrier patrolling the Arabian Sea. Plus this.
paragraphs.cn: 接下来在CNN《今晨》节目中，与伊朗战争的前线生活是怎样的？我们将带您登上在阿拉伯海巡逻的美国航空母舰。此外还有这个。

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.en: This way he can't complain that we're not giving them enough. I say make them yourself.
paragraphs.cn: 这样他就不能抱怨我们给的不够。我说让他们自己造。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Could this be the change Ukraine needs in its war with Russia? How President Trump is boosting Ukraine's air defenses. (COMMERCIAL)
paragraphs.cn: 这会是乌克兰在与俄罗斯战争中所需的改变吗？特朗普总统如何增强乌克兰的防空能力。（广告）

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So a big political win for Ukraine hand-delivered by President Trump. He announced the U.S. will be buying Ukrainian drones and more significantly will be teaming with Ukraine to co-produce Patriot missile interceptors -- a development that could significantly improve Kyiv's air defenses.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，特朗普总统亲自为乌克兰带来了一场重大的政治胜利。他宣布美国将购买乌克兰无人机，更重要的是，将与乌克兰合作共同生产爱国者导弹拦截器 -- -- 这一进展可能显著提升基辅的防空能力。

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.en: We're going to give a license to you to make Patriots. That's pretty cool, right?
paragraphs.cn: 我们将授权你们制造爱国者系统。这很酷，对吧？

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.en: This way he can't complain that we're not giving them enough. I say make them yourself. We haven't informed the company of that yet but that'll work out alright. I'm sure they'll be thrilled. But, you know, they'll be able to do it. You'll be able to figure that out. Most countries couldn't do that.
paragraphs.cn: 这样他就不能抱怨我们给的不够。我说让他们自己造。我们还没有通知那家公司，但一切都会顺利的。我相信他们会很兴奋。但是，你知道，他们有能力做到。你们能解决这个问题。大多数国家做不到这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.en: If I said that to most countries, they wouldn't know what I'm talking about. But this is a very ingenious group. And what I like about that it's a defensive -- it's a defensive situation as opposed to an offensive.
paragraphs.cn: 如果我对大多数国家这么说，他们不会明白我在说什么。但这是一个非常聪明的团队。我喜欢的是，这是防御性的 -- -- 这是防御性的情况，而不是进攻性的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Joel Rubin joins us. He's the former deputy assistant Secretary of State for Legislative Affairs under the Obama administration. Good morning, Joel. JOEL RUBIN,
paragraphs.cn: 乔尔·鲁宾加入我们。他曾是奥巴马政府时期负责立法事务的副助理国务卿。早上好，乔尔。乔尔·鲁宾：

paragraphs.en: Good morning, Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 早上好，奥蒂。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: There's actually a lot to unpack in that short statement, but I want to start with this -- the turnaround from when Trump was sitting next to Zelenskyy and telling him you don't have any cards. You can't dictate this process. What do you think has changed in the backdrop leading up to this moment?
paragraphs.cn: 实际上，那句简短声明里有很多值得解读的地方，但我想从这一点开始 -- -- 特朗普坐在泽连斯基旁边，告诉他你没有筹码，你不能主导这个过程，到现在态度转变。你认为是什么背景变化导致了这一刻？

paragraphs.speaker: Rubin
paragraphs.en: Audie, it's great to be with you. And clearly, Trump likes a winner and what he's seeing now and what the intelligence is demonstrating is that Russia is on the defensive. Ukraine is able to strike into Moscow at will. That the losses on the field -- on the ground by Russia have been significant and they're not making progress. And so President Trump -- he sees that. And you combine that -- and I've got to say that moment yesterday at NATO was really surreal.
paragraphs.cn: 奥蒂，很高兴和你连线。显然，特朗普喜欢赢家，他现在看到的以及情报显示的是俄罗斯处于守势。乌克兰能够随意打击莫斯科。俄罗斯在地面上的损失惨重，而且没有取得进展。所以特朗普总统看到了这一点。再加上 -- -- 我得说昨天在北约的那一刻真的很超现实。

paragraphs.speaker: Rubin
paragraphs.en: President Trump basically has now flipped his narrative on NATO. He now sees it as a value add to American security. And so combining that with the move on Zelenskyy, I think Trump realizes that he can gain a lot more with our allies and our friends and sees Ukraine as winning in this war.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普总统基本上已经改变了他对北约的论调。他现在认为北约对美国安全有附加价值。所以结合他对泽连斯基的态度转变，我认为特朗普意识到他可以从盟友和朋友那里获得更多，并且认为乌克兰正在赢得这场战争。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: At the same time, Ukraine is not winning. It has Russia at a standstill.
paragraphs.cn: 与此同时，乌克兰并没有赢。它只是让俄罗斯陷入僵局。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And I was surprised to hear the president use the word there "this is defensive" when Ukraine, as you mentioned, has pushed its missiles further and further deeper into Russian territory.
paragraphs.cn: 我很惊讶听到总统使用"这是防御性的"这个词，因为正如你提到的，乌克兰已经将其导弹越来越深入地推进俄罗斯领土。

paragraphs.speaker: Rubin
paragraphs.en: Yeah. So Patriots are a defensive weapons system -- a missile defense system. Now, the Russians are going to say that this is an aggression because they don't like there being any missile defense near them because that inhibits their ability to strike. But for Ukraine to hold Russia to a standstill -- that's winning because again, Russia is invading into Ukraine. But these strikes into Moscow -- that's the gamechanger.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。爱国者是防御性武器系统 -- -- 导弹防御系统。现在，俄罗斯会说这是侵略，因为他们不喜欢附近有任何导弹防御系统，因为这抑制了他们的打击能力。但对乌克兰来说，让俄罗斯陷入僵局就是胜利，因为俄罗斯正在入侵乌克兰。而这些对莫斯科的打击 -- -- 这是改变游戏规则的因素。

paragraphs.speaker: Rubin
paragraphs.en: The Russian people are noticing that the war is now coming home and that's creating pressure on Putin as well. And I think for President Trump to see Ukraine not only being able to strike inside Moscow but then, of course, to defend its territory -- if he can add this extra nudge maybe he thinks that can get Putin to a deal. I hope he's right. But certainly it is quite a turnaround with Ukraine.
paragraphs.cn: 俄罗斯人民注意到战争现在回到了本土，这也给普京带来了压力。我认为特朗普总统看到乌克兰不仅能够打击莫斯科内部，还能保卫自己的领土 -- -- 如果他再推一把，也许他认为这能让普京达成协议。我希望他是对的。但毫无疑问，对乌克兰的态度发生了巨大转变。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I want to connect the dots here because we're at this inflection point with the war with Iran --
paragraphs.cn: 我想把这里联系起来，因为我们正处于与伊朗战争的转折点 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- and there are some connections between what's happening with the war in Iran and what's happening between Ukraine and Russia. One example is drones. Just Ukraine really showcasing it is now at the forefront of innovation on drone battlefield warfare, and the U.S. now dealing with a country that can churn out drones. Is that, like, yet another aspect of this that has kind of shifted things? And then I want to ask you about sanctions.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 伊朗战争和乌克兰与俄罗斯之间发生的事情有一些联系。一个例子是无人机。乌克兰确实展示了它现在处于无人机战场创新的前沿，而美国现在正在应对一个能够大量生产无人机的国家。这是否是另一个改变局面的方面？然后我想问你关于制裁的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Rubin
paragraphs.en: Yeah. Look, you know, I was just in Israel last week and the drone wars are real. They're across the Middle East. And Ukraine, out of necessity, has perfected drone technology on the cheap. But also there's another factor as well, which is that Iran was providing drone technology to Russia to bomb into Ukraine as well. So all of these wars now -- they seem to be connecting. The technology is real. It's robust coming out of Ukraine.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。你看，我上周刚在以色列，无人机战争是真实存在的。它们遍布中东。而乌克兰出于需要，以低成本完善了无人机技术。但还有另一个因素，那就是伊朗曾向俄罗斯提供无人机技术来轰炸乌克兰。所以所有这些战争现在似乎都联系在一起了。技术是真实的，而且来自乌克兰的技术很强大。

paragraphs.speaker: Rubin
paragraphs.en: They have a value added edge frankly in the global marketplace and I think for Trump he sees that as well. And that's the future of warfare. Those drones and that technology that Ukraine has is the top of the line.
paragraphs.cn: 坦率地说，他们在全球市场上具有增值优势，我认为特朗普也看到了这一点。这就是战争的未来。乌克兰拥有的那些无人机和技术是一流的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Does this open up the channel or --
paragraphs.cn: 这是否打开了渠道 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- make it easier for Netanyahu and Israel to continue the war against Hezbollah? I mentioned this to someone yesterday but from a diplomatic point of view I'd love to hear from you what you think. Is it basically all bets are off if the New York Post is posting, you know, "Peace Out" --
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 让内塔尼亚胡和以色列更容易继续对真主党的战争？我昨天对某人提到过这一点，但从外交角度来看，我很想听听你的看法。如果《纽约邮报》刊登了"和平再见"，是不是基本上一切都不确定了？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- on the ceasefire deal?
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 关于停火协议？

paragraphs.speaker: Rubin
paragraphs.en: I mean, Audie, there's two parallel tracks on diplomacy with Iran. Of course, the ceasefire that's now in abeyance between us and Iran. And then there's the separate track, which is Israel and Lebanon government-to-government being sponsored by the State Department and Marco Rubio? That's always been a tension point in the diplomacy. So I wouldn't say that it's a guarantee that we're going to see fighting renewed inside of Lebanon because the Israeli government and the Lebanese government are talking right now.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，Audie，对伊朗的外交有两条平行的轨道。当然，目前我们与伊朗之间的停火协议处于搁置状态。还有另一条轨道，即以色列和黎巴嫩政府之间的对话，由国务院和马可·卢比奥主持？这在外交中一直是一个紧张点。所以我不认为这能保证我们会看到黎巴嫩境内重新爆发战斗，因为以色列政府和黎巴嫩政府目前正在谈判。

paragraphs.speaker: Rubin
paragraphs.en: And that actually seems to be bearing more fruit than the Iran-U.S. MOU that has obviously collapsed.
paragraphs.cn: 而且这似乎比显然已经破裂的伊朗-美国谅解备忘录更有成果。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Before I let you go, a short time left. After this NATO summit, is the U.S. in a stronger position with Europe? Is Putin going to be concerned that relationship has improved?
paragraphs.cn: 在你离开之前，还有一点时间。这次北约峰会后，美国在欧洲的地位是否更强了？普京会担心这种关系改善吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Rubin
paragraphs.en: Well, he can't be pleased, right? Like, if you're Vladimir Putin your whole goal for the last decade, frankly, if not longer has been to separate the United States from NATO and to break NATO. To break that collective security arrangement inside Europe.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，他不可能高兴，对吧？如果你是弗拉基米尔·普京，过去十年甚至更长时间里，你的全部目标就是让美国脱离北约并瓦解北约，打破欧洲内部的集体安全安排。

paragraphs.speaker: Rubin
paragraphs.en: So when the mood music -- mood music changes, as it did with President Trump yesterday where the United States and NATO and our European allies are essentially talking from the same sheet -- that is a sign to Putin that he can't break the alliance, and that is a strong position for American national security.
paragraphs.cn: 所以当氛围音乐 -- -- 氛围音乐发生变化时，就像昨天特朗普总统那样，美国、北约和我们的欧洲盟友基本上步调一致 -- -- 这对普京来说是一个信号，表明他无法瓦解这个联盟，这对美国国家安全来说是一个强有力的位置。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, Joel. Don't get any breakfast yet. I need you in the next hour. We're going to talk more --
paragraphs.cn: 好的，Joel。先别吃早餐。下一个小时我需要你。我们还要再谈 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- about some of these regional concerns. Next on CNN THIS MORNING a CNN crew aboard the USS Abraham Lincoln in the Arabian Sea. It's one of more than 20 Navy warships patrolling the waters near the Middle East. And in the next hour, President Trump promised cheaper gas prices. Is Freedom Fuel the answer? (COMMERCIAL)
paragraphs.cn: -- 关于其中一些地区关切。接下来在《CNN今晨》节目中，CNN记者登上阿拉伯海的亚伯拉罕·林肯号航母。这是在中东附近海域巡逻的20多艘海军军舰之一。而在接下来的一小时，特朗普总统承诺降低油价。自由燃料是答案吗？（广告）

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Our breaking news coverage continues now as the U.S. and Iran trade new strikes overnight. The U.S. military touting its presence in the region, posting new video of its more than 20 Navy warships patrolling the waters near the Middle East. CNN's Pam Brown was on one of those ships in the Northern Arabian Sea yesterday with the crew of the USS Abraham Lincoln. (BEGIN VIDEOTAPE)
paragraphs.cn: 我们的突发新闻持续报道，美国和伊朗一夜之间发动了新的打击。美国军方炫耀其在该地区的存在，发布了20多艘海军军舰在中东附近海域巡逻的新视频。CNN的帕姆·布朗昨天在北阿拉伯海的一艘军舰上，与亚伯拉罕·林肯号的船员在一起。（视频开始）

paragraphs.speaker: Pamela Brown
paragraphs.en: This is one of two aircraft carriers in the region. And you see behind me what's called the bridge -- the flight tower. This is where all the shots are called. And you see the planes. Here they are on this deck. There's F- 35s, F-18s, Growlers, E-2s. All of them play certain roles in the conflict. Now, what we know for sure per a U.S. official I spoke to, is that there will be defensive missions happening on the USS Abraham.
paragraphs.cn: 这是该地区的两艘航空母舰之一。在我身后的是所谓的舰桥 -- -- 飞行塔台。所有指令都在这里发出。你可以看到飞机。它们就在甲板上。有F-35、F-18、咆哮者、E-2。它们在冲突中都扮演着特定角色。现在，根据我采访的一位美国官员的说法，我们确定的是，亚伯拉罕·林肯号上将执行防御任务。

paragraphs.speaker: Pamela Brown
paragraphs.en: And, in fact, there has already been a shift in operational plans in the wake of the Iranian attacks on those vessels. This ship left port early yesterday so that it can be out here at sea and ready to go in case orders are given. So you can see some of that preparation happening right now. There is some maintenance happening over here on this helicopter. And then if we pan over here you can see they pulled out some armaments -- some missiles -- to put on the flights.
paragraphs.cn: 事实上，在伊朗袭击那些船只之后，作战计划已经发生了变化。这艘船昨天早些时候离港，以便在海上待命，随时准备在接到命令时行动。所以你可以看到一些准备工作正在进行。这里有一架直升机正在进行维护。然后如果我们把镜头移过来，可以看到他们取出了一些武器 -- -- 一些导弹 -- -- 准备装到飞机上。

paragraphs.speaker: Pamela Brown
paragraphs.en: Now I'm told that would be standard operating procedure because tomorrow is a flight day. But, of course, this takes on new meaning in the wake of the latest rhetoric from the president and the Iranians as well, so we'll have to wait and see. As one source said, it is a very dynamic situation here. Pamela Brown, CNN, from the USS Abraham Lincoln. (END VIDEOTAPE)
paragraphs.cn: 我被告知这将是标准操作程序，因为明天是飞行日。但当然，在总统和伊朗方面最新言论之后，这有了新的意义，所以我们只能拭目以待。正如一位消息人士所说，这里的情况非常动态。CNN的帕梅拉·布朗，从亚伯拉罕·林肯号报道。（视频结束）

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And I'm Audie Cornish. Stay with us. Our breaking news coverage continues right now.
paragraphs.cn: 我是奥迪·科尼什。请继续关注我们。我们的突发新闻报道现在继续。

paragraphs.speaker: Announcer
paragraphs.en: This is CNN breaking news.
paragraphs.cn: 这里是CNN突发新闻。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And the breaking news we are following, the war with Iran. New threats, new attacks. Are we in a new phase of this war?
paragraphs.cn: 我们正在追踪的突发新闻：与伊朗的战争。新的威胁，新的攻击。我们是否处于这场战争的新阶段？

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.en: When they hit, we hit back much harder. They started this. They're a little bit out of control, but they want to make a deal badly.
paragraphs.cn: 当他们打击时，我们更猛烈地回击。是他们挑起的。他们有点失控，但他们非常想达成协议。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. We're going to start with the big question this morning. Are the U.S. and Iran about to return to full-scale war? The U.S. military hit more than 90 military targets last night. President Trump posted these videos on Truth Social showing some of the attacks. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 大家早上好。我是奥迪·科尼什。我们首先从今早的大问题开始。美国和伊朗是否即将重返全面战争？美国军方昨晚打击了90多个军事目标。特朗普总统在Truth Social上发布了这些视频，展示了一些攻击。

vocabulary.en: a temporary suspension of fighting, typically one during which peace talks take place
vocabulary.excerpt: A U.S. official telling CNN the ceasefire with Iran has, quote, temporarily ceased
vocabulary.exampleCn: 一位美国官员告诉CNN，与伊朗的停火已'暂时停止'

vocabulary.en: characterized by constant change, activity, or progress
vocabulary.excerpt: the situation remains dynamic
vocabulary.exampleCn: 局势仍然动态变化

vocabulary.en: a tower used to monitor and control maritime traffic in a port or waterway
vocabulary.excerpt: showing some damage to what is a maritime control tower in a port city
vocabulary.exampleCn: 显示对一个港口城市的海上控制塔造成了一些损坏

vocabulary.en: the basic physical and organizational structures needed for the operation of a society, such as roads, bridges, and power grids, that are not military
vocabulary.excerpt: this would be a rare case where civilian infrastructure was targeted
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这将是一个罕见的针对民用基础设施的案例

vocabulary.en: a person who conducts negotiations, especially in business or politics
vocabulary.excerpt: Their top negotiator posting this warning on X
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他们的首席谈判代表在X上发布了这一警告

vocabulary.en: behavior that involves the use of force, threat, or coercion to abuse, intimidate, or aggressively dominate others
vocabulary.excerpt: America still hasn't learned that bullying and breaking promises are no longer cost-free
vocabulary.exampleCn: 美国仍然没有学到，欺凌和违背承诺不再是没有代价的

vocabulary.en: a blatant and deliberate breach of a law, rule, or agreement
vocabulary.excerpt: calling it a flagrant violation of international humanitarian law
vocabulary.exampleCn: 称其为公然违反国际人道主义法

vocabulary.en: an increase in the intensity or seriousness of something, especially a conflict
vocabulary.excerpt: this is really the most serious escalation in violence that we've seen
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这确实是我们所见过的暴力最严重的升级

vocabulary.en: a type of agreement between two or more parties, often used in international relations, that outlines the terms and details of an understanding
vocabulary.excerpt: since this memorandum of understanding was signed
vocabulary.exampleCn: 自该谅解备忘录签署以来

vocabulary.en: involving or causing sudden great damage or suffering
vocabulary.excerpt: a return to full-scale hostilities would have catastrophic consequences
vocabulary.exampleCn: 恢复全面敌对行动将产生灾难性后果

vocabulary.en: a narrow strait between the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman, a critical oil transit chokepoint
vocabulary.excerpt: It really is focusing around the Strait of Hormuz
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这确实集中在霍尔木兹海峡周围

vocabulary.en: the action of returning a military attack; counterattack
vocabulary.excerpt: Iran also claiming that it targeted Qatar in the retaliation against these U.S. military assets
vocabulary.exampleCn: 伊朗还声称，在针对这些美国军事资产的报复中，它瞄准了卡塔尔

vocabulary.en: a small naval vessel designed to detect and remove naval mines
vocabulary.excerpt: And we have deployed a minesweeper in the region
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我们已经在那个地区部署了一艘扫雷艇

vocabulary.cn: 许可环境，允许行动的环境
vocabulary.en: a situation in which conditions allow for a particular action to be taken, often with the consent of relevant parties
vocabulary.excerpt: a permissive environment is needed
vocabulary.exampleCn: 需要一个许可的环境

vocabulary.cn: 以牙还牙的，针锋相对的
vocabulary.en: a situation in which one person or group responds to another's action with a similar action
vocabulary.excerpt: in the middle of this tit-for-tat that we are seeing at this exact moment
vocabulary.exampleCn: 处于我们此刻正在看到的这种针锋相对之中

vocabulary.en: a formal movement of people accompanying a dead body to its burial or cremation
vocabulary.excerpt: the week-long funeral procession for Iran's former supreme leader nears an end

vocabulary.en: the highest-ranking political and religious authority in Iran, with ultimate power over the government
vocabulary.excerpt: the week-long funeral procession for Iran's former supreme leader

vocabulary.en: the place where a dead person is buried

vocabulary.exampleCn: 他的棺材现在正被运往最终安息地

vocabulary.excerpt: He hasn't been seen since taking the reins of the Islamic Republic of Iran
vocabulary.exampleCn: 自从接管伊朗伊斯兰共和国以来，他一直没有露面

vocabulary.en: in large numbers
vocabulary.excerpt: many people came out. They were out in force
vocabulary.exampleCn: 许多人出来了。他们大批出动

vocabulary.en: to be in charge; to make the important decisions
vocabulary.excerpt: he is the one calling the shots
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他是那个发号施令的人

vocabulary.en: left without the means to move from somewhere
vocabulary.excerpt: some 6,000 seafarers that is still stranded in the Persian Gulf
vocabulary.exampleCn: 大约6000名海员仍然被困在波斯湾

vocabulary.en: a river, canal, or other navigable channel used for travel or transport
vocabulary.excerpt: they are unable to get out of this waterway at this point
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他们目前无法离开这条水道

vocabulary.en: to keep close to something, especially a coast or border
vocabulary.excerpt: a northern route, which hugs the Iranian coast
vocabulary.exampleCn: 一条紧靠伊朗海岸的北部路线

sentences.en: A U.S. official telling CNN the ceasefire with Iran has, quote, temporarily ceased, but the situation remains dynamic.
sentences.cn: 一位美国官员告诉CNN，与伊朗的停火协议“暂时停止”，但局势仍然动态变化。
sentences.structure: 主句（A U.S. official telling CNN）+ 宾语从句（the ceasefire...ceased）+ 插入语（quote）+ 并列句（but the situation remains dynamic）
sentences.analysis: quote表示直接引用，新闻中常用；dynamic意为动态的、不断变化的，是新闻高频词。

sentences.en: And if confirmed, this would be a rare case where civilian infrastructure was targeted.
sentences.cn: 如果得到确认，这将是罕见的针对民用基础设施的案例。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（if confirmed）+ 主句（this would be a rare case）+ 定语从句（where civilian infrastructure was targeted）
sentences.analysis: if confirmed是省略结构，完整为if it is confirmed；rare case表示罕见情况；civilian infrastructure指民用基础设施。

sentences.en: Iran's military says it targeted U.S. military bases in Kuwait and Bahrain.
sentences.cn: 伊朗军方称其袭击了美国在科威特和巴林军事基地。
sentences.structure: 主句（Iran's military says）+ 宾语从句（it targeted...）
sentences.analysis: target作动词，意为瞄准、攻击；military bases军事基地。

sentences.en: Their top negotiator posting this warning on X, quote, "America still hasn't learned that bullying and breaking promises are no longer cost-free."
sentences.cn: 他们的首席谈判代表在X上发布警告称：“美国仍然没有学会，欺凌和背信不再是没有代价的。”
sentences.structure: 主句（Their top negotiator posting this warning on X）+ 插入语（quote）+ 直接引语（America still hasn't learned that...）
sentences.analysis: posting是现在分词作伴随状语；bullying and breaking promises是并列主语；cost-free意为无代价的。

sentences.en: Bahrain saying that they had intercepted several attacks, calling it a flagrant violation of international humanitarian law.
sentences.cn: 巴林称他们拦截了数次袭击，并称之为公然违反国际人道主义法的行为。
sentences.structure: 主句（Bahrain saying）+ 宾语从句（that they had intercepted...）+ 伴随状语（calling it...）
sentences.analysis: intercept意为拦截；flagrant violation公然违反；international humanitarian law国际人道主义法。

sentences.en: Now, this is really the most serious escalation in violence that we've seen since this memorandum of understanding was signed.
sentences.cn: 这是自该谅解备忘录签署以来我们看到的最严重的暴力升级。
sentences.structure: 主句（this is the most serious escalation）+ 定语从句（that we've seen）+ 时间状语从句（since...was signed）
sentences.analysis: escalation升级；memorandum of understanding谅解备忘录，常缩写为MoU。

sentences.en: The Secretary General reiterates that a return to full-scale hostilities would have catastrophic consequences for the peoples of the region, for international peace and security, and for the global economy.
sentences.cn: 秘书长重申，恢复全面敌对行动将对该地区人民、国际和平与安全以及全球经济造成灾难性后果。
sentences.structure: 主句（The Secretary General reiterates）+ 宾语从句（that a return...would have...）+ 并列介词短语（for...for...and for...）
sentences.analysis: reiterate重申；full-scale hostilities全面敌对行动；catastrophic consequences灾难性后果；三个for并列强调影响范围。

sentences.en: We are seeing the number of vessels managing to get through that critical waterway lesson because of the increase in hostilities.
sentences.cn: 由于敌对行动增加，我们看到成功通过那条关键水道的船只数量减少。
sentences.structure: 主句（We are seeing）+ 宾语（the number of vessels）+ 现在分词短语（managing to get through...）+ 原因状语（because of...）
sentences.analysis: vessels船只；critical waterway关键水道；lesson此处应为less（减少），可能是转录错误，但按原文处理。

sentences.en: On Wednesday, we had some facts and figures from marine traffic saying that, in the previous 24 hours, about 20 ships had managed to get through.
sentences.cn: 周三，我们从海上交通数据中得知，在过去24小时内，约有20艘船只成功通过。
sentences.structure: 时间状语（On Wednesday）+ 主句（we had some facts and figures）+ 现在分词短语（saying that...）+ 宾语从句（about 20 ships had managed to get through）
sentences.analysis: facts and figures事实和数据；marine traffic海上交通；manage to do设法做成。

sentences.en: We're also hearing from the U.N. Maritime Agency that they still have some 6,000 seafarers that is still stranded in the Persian Gulf since this war began, so many of them since February 28th, that they are unable to get out of this waterway at this point because of these hostilities that are ongoing.
sentences.cn: 我们还从联合国海事机构获悉，自战争开始以来，仍有约6000名海员被困在波斯湾，其中许多人自2月28日起就无法离开这条水道，原因是持续的敌对行动。
sentences.structure: 主句（We're hearing from...）+ 宾语从句（that they still have...）+ 定语从句（that is still stranded...）+ 同位语（so many of them since February 28th）+ 结果状语从句（that they are unable to get out...）+ 原因状语（because of...）
sentences.analysis: seafarers海员；stranded被困；waterway水道；ongoing持续的。

sentences.en: Al Jazeera just spoke to Germany's foreign minister, and he said that they were ready to at least take some supportive action in the Strait of Hormuz.
sentences.cn: 半岛电视台刚刚采访了德国外长，他表示他们准备至少在霍尔木兹海峡采取一些支持性行动。
sentences.structure: 并列句（Al Jazeera spoke...and he said）+ 宾语从句（that they were ready to...）
sentences.analysis: Al Jazeera半岛电视台；foreign minister外交部长；supportive action支持性行动；Strait of Hormuz霍尔木兹海峡。

sentences.en: We are urging all parties to come back to the negotiation table.
sentences.cn: 我们敦促各方回到谈判桌。
sentences.structure: 主句（We are urging）+ 宾语（all parties）+ 不定式短语（to come back to...）
sentences.analysis: urge sb. to do敦促某人做某事；negotiation table谈判桌，比喻谈判。

sentences.en: These mines are believed to be planted by Iran in the central route through the Strait of Hormuz.
sentences.cn: 这些水雷据信是伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡的中央航道上布设的。
sentences.structure: 主句（These mines are believed）+ 不定式短语（to be planted by Iran）+ 地点状语（in the central route...）
sentences.analysis: be believed to be据信是；planted布设；central route中央航道。

sentences.en: There is still a northern route, which hugs the Iranian coast, but that is the route where you have to have Iranian permission and coordination.
sentences.cn: 还有一条北部航道，紧贴伊朗海岸，但那条航道需要获得伊朗的许可和协调。
sentences.structure: There be句型（There is a northern route）+ 非限制性定语从句（which hugs...）+ 并列句（but that is the route）+ 定语从句（where you have to have...）
sentences.analysis: hug紧贴；permission and coordination许可和协调。

sentences.en: They believe that they are in control of the Strait of Hormuz, that this memorandum gives them control over the logistics, the coordination, and all ships transiting through have to coordinate with them and any that are trying to avoid that and go via the Omani route, some of them are being targeted.
sentences.cn: 他们认为自己控制着霍尔木兹海峡，这份备忘录赋予他们对物流和协调的控制权，所有过境船只都必须与他们协调，而那些试图避开并经由阿曼航道的船只，其中一些正成为攻击目标。
sentences.structure: 主句（They believe）+ 两个并列宾语从句（that they are in control...that this memorandum gives...）+ 并列句（and all ships...have to coordinate...）+ 定语从句（that are trying to avoid...）+ 主句（some of them are being targeted）
sentences.analysis: in control of控制；logistics物流；transit through过境；coordinate协调；Omani route阿曼航道；targeted被瞄准。

sentences.en: But as I say, it's hard to believe that any country, any of these European nations offering the mine-sweeping capabilities are going to want to put their vessels and their personnel in the middle of this tit-for-tat that we are seeing at this exact moment.
sentences.cn: 但正如我所说，很难相信任何国家，这些提供扫雷能力的欧洲国家，会愿意将他们的船只和人员置于我们此刻看到的这种针锋相对之中。
sentences.structure: 插入语（as I say）+ 主句（it's hard to believe）+ 宾语从句（that any country...are going to want to...）+ 定语从句（that we are seeing...）
sentences.analysis: mine-sweeping扫雷；tit-for-tat针锋相对；at this exact moment此刻。

sentences.en: This new round of strikes is coming as the week-long funeral procession for Iran's former supreme leader nears an end.
sentences.cn: 新一轮打击正值伊朗前最高领袖为期一周的葬礼接近尾声之际。
sentences.structure: 主句（This new round of strikes is coming）+ 时间状语从句（as the funeral procession nears an end）
sentences.analysis: a new round of新一轮；funeral procession葬礼队伍；nears an end接近尾声。

sentences.en: Earlier today, we were talking about this marathon funeral continued in Iraq, where mourners carried Ali Khamenei's coffin in a crowded mosque.
sentences.cn: 今天早些时候，我们谈到这场马拉松式的葬礼在伊拉克继续举行，哀悼者在一个拥挤的清真寺中抬着阿里·哈梅内伊的灵柩。
sentences.structure: 时间状语（Earlier today）+ 主句（we were talking about this marathon funeral）+ 定语从句（where mourners carried...）
sentences.analysis: marathon funeral马拉松式葬礼；mourners哀悼者；coffin灵柩；crowded mosque拥挤的清真寺。

sentences.en: The question looming over today's events, will the new Supreme Leader, Khamenei's son, make an appearance?
sentences.cn: 笼罩在今天活动上的问题是，新最高领袖、哈梅内伊的儿子会露面吗？
sentences.structure: 主句（The question looming over today's events）+ 同位语（will the new Supreme Leader...make an appearance?）
sentences.analysis: looming over笼罩；make an appearance露面。

sentences.en: One of the things, though, that the leadership here says is they say that he's fully in charge, he's fully in control.
sentences.cn: 不过，这里的领导层所说的一件事是，他们说他完全负责，完全掌控。
sentences.structure: 主句（One of the things is）+ 定语从句（that the leadership here says）+ 表语从句（they say that...）
sentences.analysis: in charge负责；in control掌控。

topics.title: 美伊冲突与停火协议
topics.content: 美国与伊朗在2026年签署了谅解备忘录（memorandum of understanding），但近期因伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡袭击船只，美国发动报复性打击，导致停火'暂时停止'。双方互相攻击，局势升级，国际社会担忧全面战争。
topics.keywords: ceasefire · memorandum of understanding · escalation · retaliation

topics.title: 霍尔木兹海峡的战略重要性
topics.content: 霍尔木兹海峡（Strait of Hormuz）是连接波斯湾和印度洋的狭窄水道，全球约20%的石油运输经过此处。伊朗曾威胁封锁海峡，目前因冲突导致航运大幅减少，约6000名海员被困。
topics.keywords: Strait of Hormuz · waterway · oil transit · stranded seafarers

topics.title: 伊朗最高领袖更替
topics.content: 伊朗前最高领袖阿里·霍梅内伊（Ali Khamenei）去世后，其子穆杰塔巴·霍梅内伊（Mojtaba Khamenei）继任，但未公开露面。葬礼持续一周，从德黑兰到伊拉克再到马什哈德，民众情绪愤怒，要求报复美国。
topics.keywords: supreme leader · Ali Khamenei · Mojtaba Khamenei · funeral procession

topics.title: 国际社会的反应与调解努力
topics.content: 联合国秘书长警告全面敌对行动将带来灾难性后果。德国外长表示愿在霍尔木兹海峡部署扫雷艇（minesweeper），但需伊朗和阿曼同意。各方呼吁回到谈判桌。
topics.keywords: United Nations · minesweeper · permissive environment · negotiation

quiz.question: 根据原文，'A U.S. official telling CNN the ceasefire with Iran has, quote, temporarily ceased' 中 'ceased' 最接近的意思是？
quiz.explanation: ceased 意为'停止'，与原文'停火暂时停止'一致。

quiz.question: 原文 'calling it a flagrant violation of international humanitarian law' 中 'flagrant' 的含义是？
quiz.explanation: flagrant 意为'公然的、明目张胆的'，强调违反行为的严重性。

quiz.question: 短语 'take the reins' 在原文 'He hasn't been seen since taking the reins of the Islamic Republic of Iran' 中的意思是？
quiz.explanation: take the reins 是固定搭配，意为'接管、掌控'，这里指穆杰塔巴接管伊朗领导权。

quiz.question: 根据报道，美国与伊朗之间的停火状态如何？
options: C. 暂时停止，局势动态
D. 被联合国强制恢复
quiz.explanation: 原文明确说'停火已暂时停止，但局势仍然动态变化'，因此选C。

quiz.question: 关于霍尔木兹海峡的航运，以下哪项描述正确？
options: A. 每天仍有110艘船通过
B. 约20艘船在24小时内通过
C. 所有船只都使用北部路线
D. 伊朗已完全封锁海峡
quiz.explanation: 原文提到'在之前的24小时内，大约20艘船设法通过'，而战前每天110艘，因此B正确。

quiz.question: 伊朗前最高领袖的葬礼中，其子穆杰塔巴·霍梅内伊的表现如何？
options: A. 公开露面并发表演讲
B. 未公开露面，仅通过书面声明沟通
C. 在伊拉克主持葬礼
quiz.explanation: 原文说'他自接管以来一直没有露面'，'仅通过书面声明沟通'，因此B正确。
